Commands:
	GET
	SET

Chat Struct:
	chat[64] chat_id,
	
	Message **mesages
	size_t count
	size_t capacity

Message Struct:
	chat_id,
	message_id,
	body,
	size_t body_length

Cache Struct:
	HashTable *chats_by_id

Get/Set Chat
Set Message


The protocol will be http like:
	command resource\r\n
	chat-id\r\n
	(optional) message-id\r\n
	(optional) content-length\r\n (needed for SET MESSAGE)
	\r\n

	The end of the header will be \r\n\r\n
	The message if passed (SET MESSAGE) will be after the EOH.
	It will be of length <content-length> in the header.

EX:
	GET CHAT\r\nchat-id: 123\r\n\r\n
	SET CHAT\r\nChat-id: 124\r\n\r\n


Handling recvs and partial recvs:
	Cases:
		Receive full header and message
		Receive full and no message
		Receive partial header and no message
		Receive the last partial message and partial next message

	Receive Buffer:
		Double the length of a normal message.

	Protocol for reading the receive buf:
		Read until \r\n\r\n
		if we receive partial: call recv again

		-We know the number of bytes we receive
		- Feed the working all the received bytes.
		- start parsing the received bytes
		- Call pop_char func and rebuild a buf while parsing 
		- read until the EOH. 
		- If GET CHAT call get function
		- If SET CHAT call the set function
		- If SET MESSAGE further parse the head to get the length
